brand.name: FreshView Services
brand.tagline: Love the Way Your Home Looks Again

offer.what: Professional exterior cleaning services: house/siding washing, paver cleaning & restoration, roof washing, and commercial power washing
offer.coreOutcome: A visibly clean, bright, cared-for property exterior — restored curb appeal without the homeowner lifting a finger
offer.pricePoint: Quote-based (no published prices); free no-obligation quote via form or phone; one-off job tickets typical of local service businesses
offer.commitment: One-off per job; repeat seasonal bookings (not a subscription)

audience.icp: Homeowners in Acworth / Marietta, GA (30–60 yo) who notice their siding, driveway, pavers, or roof has become visibly dirty and want it handled quickly and reliably
painPoints: Embarrassed by dingy siding, stained pavers, or a dark streaky roof
Too busy or physically unable to DIY pressure washing
Worried about damaging surfaces with the wrong technique or equipment
Preparing home for sale, rental, or a special event
HOA or municipality pressure to maintain property appearance

salesCycle.length: Same day to a few days — lead submits quote request, receives follow-up with pricing, books a job date
salesCycle.trial: Free no-obligation quote (no paid trial or free service)
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Quote request form or phone/text → manual follow-up call/email with pricing → customer confirms and books a date
salesCycle.topObjection: Price vs. renting equipment and DIYing it; uncertainty about trusting a stranger on/around their property

ahaMoment: The completed job walk-around — customer sees the dramatic before/after transformation of their home's exterior for the first time

retention.recurring: Seasonal repeat bookings (spring prep, fall cleanup); no formal subscription model
retention.expansion: Upselling additional services per visit — e.g., add roof washing or paver sealing to a house exterior clean
retention.churnRisk: One-and-done customers who don't think to re-book the following season; no automated re-engagement in place

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — no post-booking confirmation or pre-job prep email sequence
Lead-Magnet Nurture — no formal nurture for quote requests that don't convert immediately
Trial/Consult Conversion — quote follow-up appears to be manual with no automated drip
Booking, Reminders & No-Show — likely no automated appointment reminders or no-show recovery
Value Newsletter — no educational content cadence keeping past customers warm
Renewal, Anniversary & Expansion — no seasonal re-booking or upsell sequence
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no lapsed-customer reactivation flow
toneVoice: Friendly, warm, and reassuring — conversational without being casual. Uses relatable homeowner language ('Sound familiar?'). Emphasizes trust, local roots, and satisfaction guarantee. Not corporate or salesy.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM / CASL (local service business; no HIPAA, financial, or legal compliance constraints)
